original_text:
### [IMPORTANT] Icon Callout Box Rule A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge** � the edge runs through the badge's center so it reads as half-on / half-off the box. Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.). **Layout (explicit):** - **Container** ? the callout sits on the page/section background (usually White `#ffffff`). The blue box itself is **inset from the left by half the badge width** so the badge's outer half has room � this is the "box gets a little smaller on the left" step. Everything else stays full content width. - **Box** ? Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape. Vertically sized to its text with generous padding; the **left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap** (? `W/2 + 24px`). - **Text** ? Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered** to the box. Never let copy slide under the badge. - **Icon badge** ? a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke), **vertically centered** on the box and **horizontally centered on the box's left edge** (the edge is the badge's perpendicular bisector ? 50% over the blue, 50% over the page background). **Geometry (the part that makes it exact):** - Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside). - Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**, and place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge** (badge `left:0` inside a `padding-left:40px` wrapper ? badge center `40px` = box edge). Distance from badge center to box edge must be **`0`**. - Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height). **Corner radius (mandatory, never change):** box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order � large on **top-left + bottom-right**, near-point on **top-right + bottom-left**, a **5:1 ratio**. The white badge **mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale**, `border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal. **Drop shadow (mandatory):** the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow** so it lifts off the blue box � e.g. `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity). This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on. ## Palette reference | Token | HEX | Use here | |---|---|---| | Brand Blue | `#00529b` | Headline text + icon stroke | | Light Blue | `#E6F0F9` | Box fill | | White | `#ffffff` | Icon badge fill | | Dark Navy | `#011E45` | Drop-shadow color (low opacity) | ## MJML ```xml
These improvements occurred as early as four weeks after starting treatment, and they continued getting better through the end of the 52-week study.
``` ## Email fallback caveats (important) The 50/50 overhang relies on `position:absolute`, which Apple Mail / iOS / most web clients honor but **Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores** � there the badge drops to the top-left in normal flow and the box won't be inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across **all** clients, the brand-safe option is to **pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG** and place it via the same absolute positioning, or bake the entire badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping the text live. When that's not feasible, ship the live version above and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge) � every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical. ## Scaling note Defaults: badge size `W = 80px`, inner icon `48px`. If your real badge is a different size, keep the relationships � **inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`** � and the bisector stays exact.

relations.note: Geometry rule adds exact positioning math on top of the base composition binding.
Locks the specific radius values referenced generically in the composition rule.
Adds mandatory shadow detail to the badge described in the composition rule.
Fallback guidance addresses email-client limitations of the absolute-positioning geometry.

quotes: A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge**
Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape
Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered** to the box
a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke), **vertically centered** on the box and **horizontally centered on the box's left edge**
Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.).
Never let copy slide under the badge.

rules.intent: Standardize the icon callout proof-point component's visual composition so the badge-on-edge device renders consistently.
rules.rule_text: The Icon Callout Box (used for proof-point statements like dosing convenience or time-to-effect) is a Light Blue (#E6F0F9) accent-shape box sized to its text, sitting on a White (#ffffff) page/section background. Headline text inside the box is Brand Blue (#00529b), Nunito Sans weight 800 at ~21px/28px line-height, left-aligned and vertically centered, and must never slide under the badge. A White (#ffffff) rounded-square icon badge holding a Brand-Blue-stroke line icon straddles the box's left edge, vertically centered and horizontally centered on that edge (50% over the box, 50% over the page background).

quotes: <mj-section background-color="#ffffff" padding="16px 25px">
Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside).
Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**
Distance from badge center to box edge must be **`0`**.
inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`** � and the bisector stays exact.

rules.intent: Preserve the precise 50/50 badge-on-edge illusion at any badge scale.
rules.rule_text: The badge-on-edge geometry is exact: with badge width W (default 80px, inner icon 48px), the blue box is inset from the left by W/2 (default 40px), and the badge is positioned so its center lands exactly on the box's left edge (distance from badge center to box edge must be 0), vertically centered via a -W/2 top offset. If the badge is resized, the same relationships must hold: inset = W/2, box left-padding = W/2 + 24px, badge vertical offset = -H/2, so the bisector split stays exact.

quotes: box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;`
`border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;`
**Corner radius (mandatory, never change):**
Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

rules.intent: Keep the signature diagonal accent-shape geometry identical across box and badge for brand recognizability.
rules.rule_text: The box corner radius is locked at 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL — large on top-left+bottom-right, near-point on top-right+bottom-left, a 5:1 ratio), and the white badge mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale (20px 4px 20px 4px, also 5:1, large TL+BR). This must never be simplified to a single uniform radius, have its corners swapped, or its diagonal flipped.

quotes: box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);
the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow**
This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

rules.intent: Ensure the badge reads as a distinct layered element rather than flat artwork.
rules.rule_text: The white icon badge must always carry a subtle Dark Navy (#011E45) low-opacity drop shadow (e.g. 0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18)) so it visually lifts off the blue box; this shadow is required on every instance, since without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

quotes: Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores
pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG
every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.

rules.intent: Manage known Outlook rendering limitations without abandoning the badge-on-edge brand device.
rules.rule_text: The 50/50 badge-on-edge overhang relies on position:absolute, which Outlook desktop (Word engine) ignores — there the badge drops to the top-left in normal flow and the box loses its inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across all clients, pre-composite the white badge (shadow, accent radii, icon) as a transparent PNG placed via the same absolute positioning, or bake the badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping text live. When that isn't feasible, ship the live version and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge); every other rule (Light Blue fill, box radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) must remain identical regardless of the degrade.
